title: COMBINATION OF ANTI-CD47 ANTIBODIES AND CELL DEATH INDUCING AGENTS, AND USES THEREOF
abstract: The present disclosure relates to combinations of anti-CD47 antibodies and cell death-inducing agents. The disclosure also relates to methods for treating or ameliorating one or more symptoms of a disease, such as cancer, by administering the combination.
claims:
Claims What is claimed 1. A method for treating or delaying progression of a cancer or reducing or inhibiting tumor growth in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of a monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47, or antigen binding fragment thereof, and an effective amount of a cell death-inducing agent, wherein the cell death- inducing agent is selected from an agent that induces apoptosis, an agent that induces immunogenic cell death (ICD), and an agent that inhibits a DNA damage response pathway. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the cell death-inducing agent is an agent that induces apoptosis selected from the group consisting of: an inhibitor of BCL-2, an inhibitor of MCL-1, an inhibitor of BCL-XL, an inhibitor of MDM2, and a combination thereof. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the agent that induces apoptosis is an inhibitor of BCL-2. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the inhibitor of BCL-2 is selected from Venetoclaxâ¢, Navitoclax, or obatoclax. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the inhibitor of BCL-2 is Venetoclaxâ¢. 6. The method of claim 2, wherein the agent that induces apoptosis is an MCL-1 inhibitor. 7. The method of any one of claims 3-5, further comprising administering an effective amount of an MCL-1 inhibitor. 8. The method of any one of claims 6-7, wherein the MCL-1 inhibitor is selected from AMG176, MIK665 and AZD5991. 9. The method of claim 2, wherein the agent that induces apoptosis is a BCL-XL inhibitor. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the BCL-XL inhibitor is WEHI-539. 11. The method of claim 2, wherein the agent that induces apoptosis is an MDM2 inhibitor. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the MDM2 inhibitor is AMG232. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein the cell death-inducing agent is an agent that induces ICD. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the agent that induces ICD is selected from an anthracycline, a proteasome inhibitor, and a platinum derivative. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the agent that induces ICD is an anthracycline. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the anthracycline is selected from doxorubicin, daunorubicin, epirubicin, idarubicin, and valrubicin. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the anthracycline is doxorubicin. 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the agent that induces ICD is a proteasome inhibitor. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the proteasome inhibitor is selected from bortezomib, carfilzomib, and ixazomib. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the proteasome inhibitor is bortezomib. 21. The method of claim 14, wherein the agent that induces ICD is a platinum derivative. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the platinum derivative is selected from oxaliplatin, carboplatin, and cisplatin. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the platinum derivative is oxaliplatin. 24. The method of any one of claims 13-23, wherein the agent that induces ICD induces cell surface calreticulin. 25. The method of claim 1, wherein the cell death-inducing agent is an agent that inhibits a DNA damage response pathway. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the agent that inhibits a DNA damage response pathway is selected from an inhibitor of poly ADP ribose polymerase (PARP). 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the inhibitor of PARP is Olaparib, Niraparib or Rucaparib. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the inhibitor of PARP is Olaparib. 29. The method of claim 25, wherein the agent that inhibits a DNA damage response pathway is temozolomide. 30. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising administering an effective amount of a hypomethylating agent. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the hypomethylating agent is selected from azacitidine, and decitibine. 32. The method of claim 31, wherein the hypomethylating agent is azacitidine. 33. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising administering an effective amount of a chemotherapeutic agent. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the chemotherapeutic agent is selected from the group consisting of: topoisomerase inhibitors, bleomycin, gemcitabine, platins, taxanes, DNA alkylating agents, CHOP and fluorouracil. 35. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47, or antigen binding fragment thereof, blocks the interaction between CD47 and SIRP . 36. The method of claim 35, wherein blocking the interaction between CD47 and SIRPa induces macrophage phagocytosis of tumor cells expressing CD47. 37. The method of any one of claims 1-36, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47, or antigen binding fragment thereof, does not cause significant hemagglutination of human erythrocytes. 38. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47, or antigen binding fragment thereof, comprises: a heavy chain complementarity determining region 1 (HC CDR1) comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 5; a heavy chain complementarity determining region 2 (HC CDR2) comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 6; a heavy chain complementarity determining region 3 (HC CDR3) comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 7; a light chain complementarity determining region 1 (LC CDR1) comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 8; a light chain complementarity determining region 2 (LC CDR2) comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 9; and a light chain complementarity determining region 3 (LC CDR3) comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 10. 39. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds to human CD47, or antigen binding fragment thereof, comprises a heavy chain variable region (VH) comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 3 and a light chain variable region (VL) comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 4. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47, or antigen binding fragment thereof, is a human antibody. 41. The method of any one of claims 39 or 40, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47 comprises a wild-type human IgG4 heavy chain constant region. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein the wild-type human IgG4 heavy chain constant region comprises the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 21. 43. The method of any one of claims 1-40, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47 comprises a mutant human IgG4 heavy chain constant region, wherein the mutations are selected from a substitution at Ser228, a substitution at Leu235, a substitution at Asn297, or a combination thereof, numbering according to EU numbering. 44. The method of claim 43, wherein the mutant IgG4 heavy chain constant region comprises an S228P substitution, an L235E substitution, an N297A substitution, or a combination thereof, numbering according to EU numbering. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein the mutant IgG4 heavy chain constant region comprises an S228P substitution and an L235E substitution, numbering according to EU numbering. 46. The method of any one of claims 41-44, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47 comprises a human kappa constant region. 47. The method of claim 46, wherein the human kappa constant region comprises the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 17. 48. The method of any one of claims 1-40, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47 comprises a wild-type human IgGl heavy chain constant region or a mutant human IgGl heavy chain constant region. 49. The method of claim 48, wherein the mutant IgGl heavy chain constant region comprises an E233P substitution, an L234A or L234E substitution, an L235A substitution, an N297A substitution, or a combination thereof, numbering according to EU numbering. 51. The method of any one of claims 1-37, wherein the monoclonal antibody that specifically binds human CD47 comprises a heavy chain comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 11, SEQ ID NO: 12, SEQ ID NO: 13, or SEQ ID NO: 14; and a light chain comprising the amino acid sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 15 or SEQ ID NO: 16. 52. The method of claim 54, wherein the hematological cancer or tumor is chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL), acute lymphocytic leukemia (ALL), chronic myelogenous leukemia (CML), diffuse large cell B cell lymphoma (DLBCL), follicular lymphoma (FL), Non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, myelofibrosis, mastocytosis, mantle cell lymphoma, multiple myeloma (MM) or acute myeloid leukemia (AML). 56. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the cancer or tumor is a cancer or tumor of a tissue selected from the group consisting of lung, pancreas, breast, liver, ovary, testicle, kidney, bladder, spine, brain, cervix, endometrium, colon/rectum, anus, esophagus, gallbladder, gastrointestinal tract, skin, prostate, testicle, pituitary, stomach, uterus, vagina and thyroid. 57.
